 info	  fast)	  that	  allows	  the	  body	  to	  have	  near	   instantaneous	  proprioception.	  This	  is	  important	  for	  doing	  delicate	  tasks	  and	  preventing receptors.	  So,	  we	  contract	  the	  polar	  region	  to	  take	  the	  slack	  out	  of	   the	  central	  region	  and	  thus	  maintain	  the	  sensitivity	  of	  the γ-­‐dynamic	  muscle	  fibres.	  These	  contract	  the	  intrafusal	  muscle	  filaments	  (located	  in	  the	  poles),	  tightening	   the	  receptor	  region	  making	  it  fibres	  convey	  information	  about	  length.	  Chain	   fibres	  receive	  γ-­‐static	  motor	  input	  to	  contract	  their	  contractile	  units	  and	  make	  them	  more Temporal	  summation	  of	  excitatory	  potentials	  reaching	   threshold	  in	  motor	  neurons	   • Brief	  muscle	  contraction	  to	  oppose	  stretch	   Tendon tap • Quick stretch – dynamic length change detected by muscle spindles • Synchronous volley of many Ia afferent action potentials • Temporal summation of excitatory potentials reaching threshold in motoneurons • Causing brief muscle contraction to oppose stretch
